artists.name: Yiyam
artists.bio: Yiyam is the solo project of J. Jilenquan, a self-taught multi-instrumentalist and producer from the Northwest Arkansas Bay Area. With a DIY ethos and a penchant for genre-hopping, Yiyam's music is a chaotic blend of punk energy, psychedelic experimentation, and heartfelt vulnerability.
riyl: King Gizzard and the Lizard Wizard

releases.title: Cognitive Dissonance
releases.desc: Cognitive Dissonance is an introspective memoir that takes the listener through the experiences and internal monologues of a time cast in equal parts love and fear. The entirety of the record stands as a chronological summary of his romantic feelings as they were horribly transmogrified by unprocessed psychosexual trauma, cosmic wave anomalies, and an ambiguous source of existential dread and impending doom. 10/10 - IGN

releases.title: Concentrated Octoquan
releases.desc: This is the second release by the prolifically weird singer-songwriter-producer-CEO Yiyam, better known by his stage name J. Jilenquan. He has done it yet again, turning his silly little thoughts and feelings into sonic soundscapes by morphing tapestries of emotion into soiled rags of impulsive reactivity. He leverages the raw feelings of the human experience, from all corners of fear, love, disgust, joy, disappointment, pride, and self-deprecation for your listening pleasure. Enjoy.
tracklist: I Don't Wanna Be Cool No More

artists.name: Lonon Underground
artists.bio: Representing what is perhaps the pinnacle of stupidity in art, Lonon Underground is what it sounds like to be locked in a house with noting but medical grade Quan, a room full of synthesizers, and no obligations whatsoever. Founded by Big D. Lenoir, a 7-time divorce-nominated pothead, and his teenage burnout son Slick Willie, Lonon Underground began producing extremely potent music during the COVID-19 plandemic. Ranging in quality from Freekstyle Chicken House to Bogart Electro to Stoner Nightmare Acid Puke Rock, their stylistic reslessness is self evident. According to anthropologists, 100% of their music was made under the influence of a proprietary hallucinagenic substance produced in NBG laboratories, which was rumored to have been concocted solely out of waste printer ink and bad batch wallpaper paste. Listner disgression is advised.

releases.title: Lonon Underground
releases.desc: In the time of the great COVID-19 pandemic, whilst everyone was busy internetmaxxing and gooning to e-girls on Discord, the Lonons decided to put their brains in neutral and start spitting out some heinous tunes. Neurochemically-distorted rants about chickens, stupidity, love, hatred, and the economy spill over top of a techno spaceship as it blasts through time itself. Strap in as Big D. Lenoir and Slick Willie take you on a journey through a simultanously lo-fi and high-as-fuck brain forest. This is assuredly an Octoquan original. Enjoy.

artists.name: snowday.
artists.bio: Snowday, originally from the DFW area, is a Texan immigrant on his way through the Fayettevillager naturalization process. Part of that process has been a test of his musical ability, of which there is profound proficiency and distinction in. His ability to compose songs in a way that is both organic but technical without sounding forced will be studied for eons, even now as his soundscapes are currently limited by the production equipment found in your average music nerd's college dorm. The feelings his works exhibit are grounded, earthly, and in-touch with reality in a way that is pleasantly uncommon. This kid will take off.
